title: tagetes mirandae
intro: Tagetes mirandae, also known as the Mexican marigold or Tamaulipas marigold, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is valued for its bright yellow and orange flowers, which have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Tagetes mirandae include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and promote wound healing. Traditionally, the plant's flowers and leaves are used to make herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and topical salves, which are used to treat a variety of ailments.
